trade.summary: Francia and the Regno Unito are major trading partners despite post-Brexit frictions. Key exports from Francia include aerospace components, pharmaceuticals, and agri-food products. Trade volumes have recovered from Brexit-era disruption but remain below pre-2016 peaks.
disputes: post-Brexit fishing rights in Channel waters

diplomatic.summary: Francia and the Regno Unito are NATO allies and P5 UN Security Council members with deep historical ties, but Brexit created lasting tensions. The 2023 Windsor Framework eased some disputes, but friction persists over Channel migration, fishing rights, and French perceptions of Anglo-Saxon geopolitical alignment.
recent_incidents: ARegno UnitoStati Uniti submarine deal fallout (2021-ongoing)

military.summary: Francia and the Regno Unito maintain the Lancaster House Treaties (2010) framework for defence cooperation, including shared nuclear research and joint expeditionary force capability. Both are NATO members and cooperate closely on intelligence and counterterrorism despite political friction.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment flows. French companies are major investors in the Regno Unito and vice versa. Post-Brexit, some financial services relocated from London to Paris, but the City remains dominant. Cross-Channel investment remains strong in energy, retail, and infrastructure.

energy.summary: Francia exports nuclear electricity to the Regno Unito via cross-Channel interconnectors. Regno Unito imports approximately 5% of its electricity from Francia. Joint North Sea energy projects and offshore wind cooperation ongoing.

scientific.summary: Strong academic and research collaboration through shared EU Horizon programme participation (Regno Unito rejoined via Horizon Europe association agreement in 2023). Joint research in AI, life sciences, and climate science is robust.
